title: Romulea chloroleuca
taxonomy: Romulea chloroleuca is classified within the family Iridaceae, which includes irises and related species. This genus, Romulea, is a group of small, cormous flowering plants commonly known as "button irises" or "South African irises." Romulea chloroleuca falls under the subgenus Romuleella. It is further classified as Romulea chloroleuca (Schltr.) Goldblatt & J.C.Manning. The species is native to the southern part of the African continent.

intro: Romulea chloroleuca, also known as the yellow-leafed Romulea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. Traditionally, Romulea chloroleuca has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Romulea chloroleuca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Romulea chloroleuca is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, and is commonly used to treat respiratory conditions, skin infections, and wounds.
